what_it_does: Defines a systemd timer unit that periodically triggers the company-routine-completion_poke.service every 1800 seconds after boot and after the timer itself becomes active, with persistence enabled, and binds the timer to company.target.
observations: Timer cadence is set to 1800 seconds via OnUnitActiveSec and OnBootSec.
Persistent=true ensures missed triggers run after downtime.
The timer unit is WantedBy=company.target rather than timers.target.
Description labels it as routine 'completion_poke' under schedule arm S-R9.1.
standouts: Bound to company.target for lifecycle coupling with the Company instead of the standard timers.target.
conventions: Follows systemd unit file section ordering: [Unit], [Timer], [Install].
Uses generated path indicating machine-produced artifact.
Names timer file to match the service it triggers (company-routine-completion_poke).
Comment explains the non-standard WantedBy choice.
notes: A generated 30-minute persistent systemd timer tied to company.target for the completion_poke routine.
questions: What is the exact semantics of 'S-R9.1 schedule arm'?
What does the completion_poke routine do when the service fires?
Is company.target defined and started as part of the system boot?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the timer configuration: it triggers the named service every 1800 seconds and binds to company.target.
apparent_themes: Operational automation through scheduled systemd timers.
Lifecycle coupling of generated units to a company-wide target.
Persistence and reliability of periodic tasks.
intention_signals: It appears designed to keep a routine operational task running on a regular, reliable cadence while making the timer visible and managed as part of the Company service collection.
novelty: The explicit binding of a generated routine timer to a custom company.target instead of the default timers.target, with a documented rationale in the unit file.
contribution: Provides a declarative, persistent 30-minute trigger for the completion_poke service and integrates it into the company.target lifecycle so it is treated as a managed Company member.
summary_for_embedding: A generated systemd timer unit that activates company-routine-completion_poke.service every 1800 seconds, with persistence and WantedBy=company.target.
intention_for_embedding: To schedule the completion_poke routine reliably on a half-hour cadence as a managed member of the Company target.
